choices.label: B
choices.text: Instruct the family to ensure that the patient drinks plenty of fluids and performs oral hygiene at least three times daily.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Instruct the family to hold the next dose of the drug and have the patient seen by the prescriber as soon as possible.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Emphasize the family that this drug cannot be stopped quickly and to gradually reduce the dose over a 2- to 3-week period.

questions.rationale: The patient’s behavior of tongue rolling and lip smacking are symptoms of tardive dyskinesia associated with chlorpromazine therapy. This neurologic problem can become permanent if the patient remains on the drug long-term. Chlorpromazine is an older antipsychotic drug and the patient may respond better to a newer one. Although stopping the drug quickly can cause nausea, vomiting, and dizziness, these effects are not life threatening.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 446

questions.stem: An older patient taking lithium carbonate (Eskalith) for 2 months has all of the following conditions. Which finding should you report to the prescriber as a possible side effect that may require a change of drug or drug dosage?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dry mouth

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increased belching after meals

choices.label: C
choices.text: Weight loss of 6 lb in 1 week

choices.label: D
choices.text: International normalized ratio (INR) of 1.1

questions.rationale: Lithium can cause a type of diabetes insipidus in which the patient has an excessive urine output that can lead to dehydration. A 6 lb weight loss is significant and should be reported to the prescriber. The patient may be at risk for dehydration.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 447

questions.stem: Four hours after receiving risperidone (Risperdal), the patient on the psychiatric unit has a temperature elevation of 2o F. What is your best action?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Administer the next dose of the drug as prescribed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Assess the patient for other signs and symptoms of infection.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hold the next drug dose and notify the prescriber immediately.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Attempt to arouse the patient from sleep by gently shaking his or her arm.

questions.rationale: A rapid temperature elevation is often the first sign of an adverse reaction called neuroleptic malignant syndrome. This reaction involves the autonomic nervous system and is potentially life threatening. Until the cause of the temperature elevation is identified, further drug dosages should be withheld.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 446

questions.stem: Which food or beverage should you teach a patient who is prescribed quetiapine (Seroquel) to avoid?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Coffee and caffeine

choices.label: B
choices.text: Fresh berries and vegetables

choices.label: C
choices.text: Aged cheese and smoked meat

choices.label: D
choices.text: Grapefruit and grapefruit juice

questions.rationale: Grapefruit and grapefruit juice interact with the enzymes that metabolize quetiapine, causing the blood drug levels to increase. This action increases the risks for side effects and adverse reactions. Patients taking this drug should avoid grapefruit and grapefruit juice.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 447

questions.stem: Which safety precaution is most important to teach an older adult who is prescribed chlorpromazine (Thorazine)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: “Avoid going barefoot, even in the house.”

choices.label: B
choices.text: “Change positions slowly when rising to stand.”

choices.label: C
choices.text: “Drink plenty of water to avoid having to strain during a bowel movement.”

choices.label: D
choices.text: “Use a thermometer to check the temperature of your bath water to avoid a scald injury.”

questions.rationale: Chlorpromazine can cause an unsafe drop in blood pressure when the patient moves from a lying or sitting position to a standing position. This can cause dizziness and an increased risk for falls, especially among older adults. Changing positions slowly reduces the rapid drop in blood pressure.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 447

questions.stem: A patient who has been prescribed prochlorperazine (Compazine) calls the clinic and reports pink-tinged urine. What is your best action?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ask if the patient consumed grapefruit or grapefruit juice during the last 24 hours.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remind the patient to drink more water to prevent this drug from damaging the kidneys.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reassure the patient that this is an expected side effect of the drug and needs no action.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Instruct the patient to hold the next drug dose and notify the prescriber immediately.

questions.rationale: This drug usually changes urine color to pink or reddish-brown. It is not an indication of bleeding or of any kidney problem. No action needs to be taken other than reassuring the patient about this change.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 446

questions.stem: A patient is prescribed 30 mg of chlorpromazine (Thorazine) intramuscularly. The drug available is 25 mg/mL. How many milliliters will you draw into the syringe?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 0.8 mL

choices.label: B
choices.text: 1 mL

choices.label: C
choices.text: 1.2 mL

choices.label: D
choices.text: 1.4 mL

questions.rationale: Want 30 mg in X mL, Have 25 mg in 1 mL. 30/25 = 1.2  1 mL = 1.2 mL.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 445

questions.stem: A patient is prescribed Ziprasidone (Geodon), 80 mg twice a day. Tablets from the pharmacy are 20 mg per tablet. How many tablets will you give for each dose?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 2 tablets

choices.label: B
choices.text: 4 tablets

choices.label: C
choices.text: 6 tablets

choices.label: D
choices.text: 8 tablets

questions.rationale: Want 80 mg in X tablets, Have 20 mg in 1 tablet = 80/20 = 4 tablets.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 445

questions.stem: A patient prescribed fluoxetine (Prozac) develops anxiety, diaphoresis, confusion, tremors, restlessness, and a heart rate of 114 per minute. What adverse effect do you recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allergic reaction

choices.label: B
choices.text: Angioedema

choices.label: C
choices.text: Serotonin syndrome

choices.label: D
choices.text: Stevens-Johnson syndrome

questions.rationale: Fluoxetine (Prozac) is an SSRI drug. Serotonin syndrome is a rare adverse effect that occurs when levels of serotonin are very high. Signs and symptoms of serotonin syndrome include anxiety, agitation, sweating, confusion, tremors, restlessness, lack of coordination, and rapid heart rate.
questions.metadata: DIF:  Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 437 ADVANCED CONCEPTS

questions.stem: Which are common treatment methods for depression? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Counseling

choices.label: B
choices.text: Surgery

choices.label: C
choices.text: Group meetings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Psychotherapy

choices.label: E
choices.text: Shock therapy

choices.label: F
choices.text: Drug therapy with lithium carbonate

questions.rationale: The most common treatments for depression include counseling or psychotherapy and antidepressants drugs.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 433

questions.stem: A patient has been prescribed amitriptyline (Elavil). What should you plan teach this patient? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: “Smoking cigarettes can decrease the effectiveness of amitriptyline in treating your depression.”

choices.label: B
choices.text: “Check your pulse every day and report any irregular rhythms to your prescriber right away.”

choices.label: C
choices.text: “Amitriptyline should help to improve your depression symptoms within 7 to 14 days.”

choices.label: D
choices.text: “Avoid driving or operating dangerous equipment until you know how the drug will affect you.”

choices.label: E
choices.text: “Be sure to include foods with lots of fiber in your diet to prevent the side effect of constipation.”

choices.label: F
choices.text: “Tell the patient that this drug can cause urine to appear orange or red.”

questions.rationale: Amitriptyline is a tricyclic antidepressant (TCA). TCA drugs may take anywhere from 2 to 8 weeks to improve depression symptoms. Cigarette smoking can decrease the effectiveness of TCA drugs. Common side effects of TCAs include lethargy, sedation, drowsiness, fatigue, blurred vision, dry mouth, hypotension, and constipation. Additionally, an adverse effect of these drugs that can occur is abnormal heart rhythms.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: pp. 437-438

questions.stem: What are common anxiety disorders? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dysthymia

choices.label: B
choices.text: Panic disorder

choices.label: C
choices.text: Bipolar disorder

choices.label: D
choices.text: Phobic disorder

choices.label: E
choices.text: Obsessive-compulsive disorder

choices.label: F
choices.text: Depression

questions.rationale: Common anxiety disorders include panic disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, phobic disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, and posttraumatic stress disorder. Dysthymia and bipolar disorder are common forms of depression.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 438

questions.stem: A patient with anxiety is prescribed oxazepam (Serax). What safety measures must you teach a patient taking this drug? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Call for help when getting out of bed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Use a walker for ambulation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Assess gait for steadiness.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Change positions slowly.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Take two tablets whenever a dose is missed.

choices.label: F
choices.text: Always use a bedside commode, even at home.

questions.rationale: Oxazepam is a benzodiazepine. Common side effects of these drugs include sedation, sleepiness, depression, light-headedness, ataxia, and unsteadiness. The patient’s gait must always be assessed for steadiness when these drugs are prescribed. Patients may need assistance getting out of bed, changing positions, or walking, but do not necessarily need a walker. Patients should be taught not to take double doses of any drug because this could cause drug overdose with severe side effects or adverse effects. A bedside commode is not necessary as long as the patient receives assistance getting out of bed.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 442

questions.stem: An older adult prescribed clozapine (Clozaril) tells you that his last bowel movement was 5 days ago. Which instructions would you share with this patient? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use a bedpan or bedside commode.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Drink extra fluids with meals and throughout the day.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Take the stool softener ordered by your prescriber.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase exercise activity during the day.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Take a daily multivitamin.

choices.label: F
choices.text: Consume lots of vegetables and fruits in your diet.

questions.rationale: Clozapine (Clozaril) is a drug used to treat psychosis. One of the side effects is constipation. After giving this drug, be sure to monitor bowel function. Tell patients to monitor bowel function and to increase activity, fluid intake, and bulk foods to prevent constipation.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 445 Workman & LaCharity: Understanding Pharmacology: Essentials for Medication Safety, 3rd Edition BASIC CONCEPTS

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: What is the most serious adverse effect of hormone replacement therapy for menopause?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Pregnancy

choices.label: B
choices.text: Blood clots

choices.label: C
choices.text: Miscarriage

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decreased mental concentration

questions.rationale: Hormone replacement therapy for perimenopausal symptoms uses different types of estrogens and sometimes progesterone. Exogenous use of these drugs increases the risk for forming blood clots where they are not needed, leading to the potential venous thromboembolism complications of deep vein thrombosis, strokes, and heart attacks.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 498

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which hormone levels in the blood are reduced as a result of taking hormone replacement therapy for menopause?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Estrogen and progesterone

choices.label: B
choices.text: Estrogen and follicle stimulating hormone

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gonadotrophin releasing hormone and progesterone

choices.label: D
choices.text: Follicle stimulating hormone and gonadotrophin releasing hormone

questions.rationale: The blood levels of estrogen are increased during hormone replacement therapy, which reduces the release of gonadotrophin releasing hormone from the brain. With low levels of gonadotrophin releasing hormone, the anterior pituitary gland greatly reduces secretion of follicle stimulating hormone.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 494

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which side effects are most common among women taking hormone replacement therapy for menopause?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Breast tenderness and fluid retention

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increased vaginal dryness and constipation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea

choices.label: D
choices.text: Skin itching and dark-colored urine

questions.rationale: The most common side effects of hormone replacement therapy for perimenopausal symptoms are breast tenderness, breakthrough bleeding, fluid retention, weight gain, and acne. Vaginal dryness is reduced, not increased. Skin itching is reduced, not increased. Dark urine would be a symptom of the adverse effect of liver toxicity. These drugs are not commonly a cause of any gastrointestinal problems.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 496

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which brand name oral contraceptive contains drospirenone?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Camila

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ovral

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yasmin

choices.label: D
choices.text: Zovia

questions.rationale: The brand of oral contraceptive known as Yasmin is a combination of drospirenone and ethinyl estradiol.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 497, Table 32-1

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which female hormone supports pregnancy by directly maintaining the thickened uterine lining?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Estrogen

choices.label: B
choices.text: Progesterone

choices.label: C
choices.text: Luteinizing hormone

choices.label: D
choices.text: Follicle stimulating hormone

questions.rationale: Estrogen increases the thickness of the uterine lining and then progesterone maintains this lining making the uterus ready for implantation. Luteinizing hormone stimulates ovulation and follicle stimulating hormone causes the ovary to produce estrogen.
questions.metadata: DIF:  Cognitive Level: Remembering    REF:  p. 493

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which type of oral contraceptive drug must be taken every day of the month to be effective?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An estrogen-progestin combination

choices.label: B
choices.text: An estrogen only “mini” pill

choices.label: C
choices.text: A progestin-drospirenone combination

choices.label: D
choices.text: A progestin only “mini” pill

questions.rationale: Progestin only mini pills contain very low concentrations of only one hormone. Because the hormone levels are low, they must be taken daily, without any time off, to be effective.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 498

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A woman taking oral contraceptives reports the following changes. Which one is an expected side effect and requires no intervention?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Calf swelling

choices.label: B
choices.text: Severe constipation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increased breast size

choices.label: D
choices.text: Heavier menstrual periods

questions.rationale: The hormones in oral contraceptives increase the growth of breast ductal tissue, often resulting in an enlargement of the breasts. This change is not pathologic and does not require intervention. Severe constipation is not related to oral contraceptive use. Calf swelling may indicate a blood clot and is a symptom that must be explored further. Most women have lighter menstrual periods.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Remembering REF: p. 498 ADVANCED CONCEPTS

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A patient who has just been prescribed estrogen-based hormone replacement therapy (HRT) also has all of the following health problems. For which problem should you check with the prescriber to determine whether the patient should take HRT? Asthma a.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hypertension

choices.label: C
choices.text: Osteoporosis

choices.label: D
choices.text: Diabetes mellitus

questions.rationale: Common side effects of menopausal HRT include breast tenderness, breakthrough bleeding, fluid retention weight gain, and acne. These occur with conjugated estrogen alone and when combined with progesterone. Fluid retention can cause or worsen hypertension. If the patient’s hypertension is not well controlled, she should not be taking HRT.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 498

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which laboratory finding in a perimenopausal woman who takes Premarin should be reported to the prescriber?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Random blood glucose 116 mg/dL

choices.label: B
choices.text: Potassium 4.8 mEq/L

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sodium 146 mEq/L

choices.label: D
choices.text: Hematocrit 27%

questions.rationale: Premarin taken long-term can thicken the uterine lining and lead to severe dysfunctional uterine bleeding. A hematocrit of 27% is way below normal.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 496

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A patient calls to report that her health care provider prescribed Premphase daily and her new bottle of pills says Prempro. What is your best advice?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Remind her that Prempro is just another brand of Premphase.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Instruct her to take the new bottle back to the pharmacy for replacement with Premphase.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ask her whether she has already taken one of the Prempro and if she has to go to the nearest emergency department.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Instruct her to call her health care provider and request that the prescription be changed to Prempro 0.3 mg daily.

questions.rationale: Prempro and Premphase are sound alike drugs both of which are used to treat perimenopausal symptoms. They can be easily confused; however, because the amounts of hormone are different, these drugs cannot be interchanged.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 494

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: How does estrogen-based hormone replacement therapy (HRT) reduce perimenopausal symptoms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By inhibiting ovulation and implantation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: By making periods more regular and predictable.

choices.label: C
choices.text: By inhibiting excessive secretion of follicle stimulating hormone.

choices.label: D
choices.text: By increasing the rate of normal involution and menopausal cessation.

questions.rationale: When the glandular cells of the ovary shrink, they no longer produce normal levels of estrogen. The decreased blood levels of estrogen trigger the brain to secrete GnRH, which then triggers the pituitary gland to secrete follicle stimulating hormone (FSH). Before menopause the FSH acts on the ovary and causes ovarian cells to secrete estrogen, which then inhibits the pathway through negative feedback. With nonfunctional ovarian cells unable to respond to FSH by increasing estrogen secretion, this pathway is disrupted for a time. The continued low blood levels of estrogen constantly stimulate the brain to secrete gonadotrophin releasing hormone (GnRH) in large amounts, resulting in the secretion of very large amounts of FSH. This extra FSH is useless because the ovary cannot respond to it and it has effects on other body tissues. The estrogen taken at this time suppressed GnRH and prevents excessive secretion of FSH, thereby reducing unpleasant symptoms.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 494

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A patient prescribed to take the oral contraceptive with the brand name Ortho Tri-Cyclen asks whether she should stop taking the pill for the 10 days that her husband is out of town on business. What is your best advice?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, this drug’s protection against pregnancy requires taking the drug as prescribed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, the hormonal confusion this would cause could result in an increased risk for blood clots.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, if you would normally have your period any time during the 10 days that your husband is away.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, it is very important that your body experience drug-free period from time to time to ensure optimal drug effect.

questions.rationale: Hormonal contraceptives must be taken exactly as prescribed in order to prevent pregnancy. Even a 2 day miss of the drugs increases the risk for pregnancy.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 498

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A woman who wants to use the oral contraceptive drospirenone ethinyl estradiol (Yasmin) is prescribed to take these other drug types daily. Which drug makes Yasmin a poor choice for her?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A corticosteroid inhaler

choices.label: B
choices.text: A nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug

choices.label: C
choices.text: An angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor

choices.label: D
choices.text: A combination opioid receptor agonist/antagonist agent

questions.rationale: Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors increase the reabsorption of potassium. Oral contraceptives that contain drospirenone also increase blood potassium levels. These two drugs should not be taken together.
questions.metadata: DIF:  Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 498

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A woman who wants to take hormone replacement therapy (HRT) for perimenopausal discomfort expresses concern because her father died of a heart attack at age 78. What is your best response?

choices.label: A
choices.text: “If you have an electrocardiogram (ECG) performed every 6 months while you are on HRT, your risk for a heart attack will be reduced.”

choices.label: B
choices.text: “The risk for a heart attack while taking HRT is slightly higher, which is why the therapy is prescribed for as short a time period as possible.”

choices.label: C
choices.text: “Because it was your father who had the heart attack rather than your mother, you do not need to worry.”

choices.label: D
choices.text: “You are right to be concerned and are not a good candidate for this form of therapy.”

questions.rationale: Recent studies indicate that women taking estrogen -based HRT have a slightly higher incidence of myocardial infarction (heart attack). For this reason estrogen-based HRT is not recommended for long-term therapy. If she has no other risk factors for a heart attack, she can be considered for HRT. An ECG does not protect against heart attack. The genetic risks for conditions that increase the risk for a heart attack are not gender-based.
questions.metadata: DIF:  Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 496 ADVANCED CONCEPTS

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which statements about perimenopausal hormone replacement therapy (HRT) are true? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Insomnia is a common side effect.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They slightly increase the risk for heart attacks.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They increase the chances of becoming pregnant.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Women taking them should avoid caffeine.

choices.label: E
choices.text: They are prescribed to prevent osteoporosis.

choices.label: F
choices.text: Weight may increase during therapy. g They should be used only by women who are sexually active. h They cannot be used by anyone who took oral contraceptives for more than 5 years.

questions.rationale: HRT does slightly increase the risk for heart attacks and increases the risk for clot formation. Although osteoporosis can be delayed by HRT, because of the heart attack and blood clot risk, they are not prescribed to prevent osteoporosis. Insomnia is a menopause problem. The low dose of estrogen helps prevent ovulation and does not increase the chances for becoming pregnant. HRT does cause water and fluid retention, which often causes some degree of weight gain and hypertension. Patients are prescribed HRT to control perimenopausal symptoms regardless of sexual activity. Caffeine restriction is not needed. A woman who took oral contraceptives for more than 5 years and had no clotting problems with them, can take HRT when it is needed.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: pp. 494-496

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: Which precautions are important to teach a patient who is taking hormone replacement therapy (HRT) to reduce menopausal discomfort? (select all that apply)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use oral contraceptives.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Avoid cigarette smoking.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Avoid drinking alcoholic beverages.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Obtain a yearly influenza vaccination.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Perform monthly breast self-examinations.

choices.label: F
choices.text: Go to the hospital immediately if chest pains or symptoms of a stroke occur.

questions.rationale: HRT with estrogens increases the risk for clot formation that could cause venous thromboembolism, stroke, and heart attack. The risk is higher if the patient also smokes. Patients taking HRT should avoid cigarette smoking and should seek medical help immediately if chest pain or symptoms of a stroke occur. Hormones like estrogen can make breast cancers grow faster. The patient should perform monthly breast self-examinations to determine the presence of any lumps that could be cancerous. Although a yearly influenza vaccination is a good health promotion practice, HRT does not increase the risk for influenza. Oral contraceptives should not be used with HRT. There is no contraindication for drinking alcohol (in moderation) while taking HRT.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Understanding REF: p. 496 ADVANCED CONCEPTS

questions.chapter_title: Drug Therapy for Female Reproductive Issues
questions.stem: A woman who is hospitalized overnight after surgery usually takes Premarin 2.5 mg daily for control of perimenopausal symptoms. Her prescriber orders a one-time dose of Premarin 2.5 mg orally as soon as possible. The drug on hand is Premarin 0.625 mg/tablet. How many tablets should you administer?
questions.rationale: Want 2.5 mg, Have 0.625 mg/tablet. 2. 5 mg/0.625 mg/tablet = 4 tablets.
questions.metadata: DIF: Cognitive Level: Applying or Higher REF: p. 495
